business.headline: Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

8th Pay Commission in Lucknow: Why salaries, pensions and fitment factor are in focusWith the 8th Pay Commission holding fresh talks in Lucknow, expectations around pay revisions and pension increases are once again in focus. The discussions could help shape the recommendations that follow.

Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

8th Pay Commission: What pensioners and family pension beneficiaries can expectThe 8th Pay Commission has begun consultations in Lucknow with unions, associations and department representatives. Its recommendations could shape pensions, family pensions and retirement security for millions.

Can Calcutta Stock Exchange make a comeback? Bengal govt proposes revival planAfter years of uncertainty and legal hurdles, the Calcutta Stock Exchange has received a fresh lifeline from the Bengal government. Can the revival proposal help bring trading back to the exchange?

India wants lower tariffs in US trade deal; hopes to sign 1st phase before July 24India is pressing the United States for lower tariffs and better market access in the proposed trade pact. The talks also carry wider significance as both sides weigh sensitive sectors and broader strategic cooperation.

Old vs new tax regime: How many times can you change your choice?Old or new tax regime? It's a choice many taxpayers revisit every year. But before making the switch, it's important to know how often you're actually allowed to change your tax regime.

Will Jio IPO really unlock big gains for Reliance investors?Mukesh Ambani has confirmed Jio Platforms' IPO filing at Reliance's AGM. Analysts say the listing may improve transparency but not automatically spark a sharp Reliance rerating.

Could you get an income tax notice by June 30? Don't panic, here's how to respondJune 30 may not be on every taxpayer's radar, but it is an important date for those who have already filed their returns. Here's who could receive a scrutiny notice and how to respond if one arrives.

Reliance shares gain over 2%: Why is RIL stock rising today?Reliance Industries shares rose after Jio Platforms filed its draft papers for an IPO. The move sharpened focus on value unlocking and the group's next growth engines.

Can Jio become India's biggest IPO ever? Here's how it compares to top 5 IPOsIf current estimates hold true, the JIO IPO could raise as much as $4 billion, or around Rs 37,000 crore. That would comfortably surpass Hyundai Motor India's Rs 27,859 crore IPO, which currently holds the record as the largest public issue in Indian history.

NSE vs Jio IPO: How do the two giants compare?NSE has filed its DRHP, while Jio Platforms is set to file its prospectus with Sebi. The twin moves bring India's most-awaited IPOs closer and spotlight financialisation and digitalisation.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IT stocks gainThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 404.20 points, or 0.53%, to 77,207.10 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 117.25 points, or 0.49%, to 24,130.35.

The taxman is using AI. These ITR mistakes could get flaggedTax experts have listed eight common ITR filing mistakes that can draw the department's attention. With AI-led data matching expanding, even small mismatches can delay refunds or trigger notices.

Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

entertainment.headline: Samantha's pregnancy comes at a beautiful time: Maa Inti Bangaaram director Nandini Reddy said Samantha Ruth Prabhu's pregnancy comes as Maa Inti Bangaaram turns successful. Her remarks followed viral speculation after the actor's appearance at the film's success meet.

Ektaa Kapoor reveals why Kangana Ranaut won't host Lock Upp on Netflix Ektaa Kapoor said Kangana Ranaut is not returning to Lock Upp because the show has been completely revamped. The Netflix move and format reset give the new season a fresh identity.

Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston's rise, dies at 94 kicker - Clive Davis, the music executive who helped shape generations of stars, died on Monday at the age of 94. His six-decade career left a lasting imprint on pop, rock, jazz and R&B.

Janhvi Kapoor's quirky nightsuit features a cheeky nod to boyfriend Shikhar Pahariya A viral photo of Janhvi Kapoor wearing a nightsuit featuring a cheeky message for Shikhar Pahariya has grabbed everyone's attention. The picture has once again sparked discussion around the subtle yet affectionate references the couple have made to each other over the years.

Actor Milly Alcock on Supergirl's sexuality: She probably goes both ways Supergirl actor Milly Alcock said she sees Kara Zor-El as someone who probably goes both ways. Her remarks add to ongoing discussion among fans about how Superman's cousin will be portrayed in the new DC Universe.

Disha Patani bikini shots, Kashmir reference among 18 edits in Welcome to The Jungle Welcome to the Jungle received a UA 16+ certificate after the censor board ordered 18 cuts and changes. The revisions removed politically sensitive dialogue and trimmed sensual visuals ahead of the film's June 26 release.
Not Alone: Timothee Chalamet, Selena Gomez unite for alien animated film Timothee Chalamet and Selena Gomez will lead an animated film Not Alone. The film will hit theatres in April 2027.

Sharmila Tagore, Sara Ali Khan join Pak star Kubra Khan's London birthday bash Sara Ali Khan and Sharmila Tagore were celebrating Pakistani actor Kubra Khan's birthday in London. A video from the celebration has gone viral.

My Wife's An Alpha: Ranbir Kapoor turns his T-shirt into Alia's cutest billboard Ranbir Kapoor stepped out in Mumbai wearing a T-shirt that nodded to Alia Bhatt's film Alpha. The brief sighting quickly drew fan praise and added to the film's growing buzz.

Mahaprabhu Jagannath trailer debuts before 10,000 devotees: Tale of faith and unity The trailer of Mahaprabhu Jagannath was unveiled by Pujya Shri Indresh Upadhyay Maharaj Ji during Shrimad Bhagwat Katha in Cuttack before more than 10,000 devotees. The film narrates the story of Lord Vishnu's incarnation as Jagannath to save his devotees from the demon, Aham.

Baby Do Die Do trailer: Huma Qureshi is a deaf-mute hitwoman on a mission The trailer of Baby Do Die Do starring Huma Qureshi is out. Slated for July 3 release, the trailer hints at betrayals, buried trauma and an identity crisis.

Om Puri's first wife Seema Kapoor recalls his affair, losing unborn child Writer and filmmaker Seema Kapoor recalled how Om Puri's affair shattered their marriage while she was pregnant. She said the loss of her child, divorce papers and her decision to avoid a legal fight made the period harder.

Batwara 1947: Did you know Sunny Deol film is based on a play banned in Pakistan? Amid the curiosity around Sunny Deol's upcoming film Batwara 1947, not many would know that it is based on a play that was reportedly banned in Pakistan.

Vijay's first question after oath was for his father, says CM's mother Shoba Vijay's mother looked back at the emotional moment that followed his swearing-in as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and the long journey that led there. The actor-politician is celebrating his 52nd birthday on June 22.

What if it was your daughter? Orry challenges idea of gender-neutral bathrooms A podcast exchange between Orry and content creator Kavya Karnatac on gender-neutral bathrooms has gone viral. The clip has fuelled an online debate.

House of the Dragon 3: The shocking incest twist that leaves even GoT fans stunned A shocking mother-son kiss in the House of the Dragon Season 3 premiere has left viewers stunned. Here's why the controversial twist is dominating discussions and what it means for the Targaryen dynasty.
Top 7 classic Indian TV shows available on OTT From Shaktimaan and Shaka Laka Boom Boom to Sarabhai vs Sarabhai and Malgudi Days, several iconic Indian TV shows are now available on OTT platforms.

In pictures: The 10 scenes that defined House of the Dragon's season 3 premiere House of the Dragon returns with a dramatic premiere featuring Aegon II's escape, Alicent's failed scheme, a shocking confrontation with Aemond and the devastating Battle of the Gullet that changes the course of the war.

Vijay turns 52: Wishes pour in from PM Modi, Rahul Gandhi, MK Stalin, Kamal Haasan Vijay's birthday drew messages from political leaders, film personalities and fans across social media. The celebrations also renewed focus on Jana Nayagan, which is still awaiting final CBFC certification.

general news.headline: 12 Indians among 13 killed in Qatar gas plant explosion, 66 injuredThe explosion occurred at the Barzan gas supply facility, part of the sprawling Ras Laffan Industrial City, the country's main LNG production and export hub.

France vs Iraq Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Mbappe in focus as Les Bleus eye Round Of 32 entryFrance (FRA) vs Iraq (IRQ) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe will be in focus once again as France eye a place in the Round Of 32 when they take on Iraq. France started off with a 3-1 win against Senegal while Iraq were beaten 4-1 by Norway.

15 killed in fire at Lucknow building housing animation studio  UpdatesA pet shop, an animation centre and a building engulfed in flames. The Lucknow fire triggered a massive rescue effort before ending in tragedy for several lives.

The inevitable Messi-ah breaks World Cup record to send Argentina into knockoutsFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi missed an early penalty but still ended the night with two goals and another World Cup record. The Argentina captain became the tournament's all-time leading scorer as the reigning champions booked their place in the knockout rounds.

Students waited a month for NEET re-test. Couldn't they be spared 2 minutes, parents askThe NEET re-test gave the examination system a second chance after the paper leak controversy. But for some students, reaching minutes late because of rain, traffic, accidents or document confusion meant there was no second chance at all.

Who's paying Iran $300 billion? Rubio heads to Gulf as allies seek answers on dealMarco Rubio is visiting Gulf states as US allies press for clarity on the Iran memorandum. The focus is on a proposed $300 billion package, frozen assets and security risks around Hormuz.

CRED founder Kunal Shah to become global CEO of WhatsAppKunal Shah, who also happens to be the founder of CRED, will take charge of WhatsApp globally. Meta has announced that Shah will become the next Head of WhatsApp, succeeding Will Cathcart. Here are all the details.

Messi becomes World Cup's top goalscorer, shatters multiple records on the wayLionel Messi put Argentina ahead against Austria and became the World Cup's all-time top goalscorer. The 38th-minute strike pushed him past Miroslav Klose and added another layer to his record-laden career.

Who is Andy Burnham, King of the North, frontrunner for UK PM's post?Keir Starmer's resignation as British Prime Minister and Labour Party leader has triggered a search for his successor. Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham, who won a parliamentary by-election last week, is being seen as the frontrunner. Who's Burnham, and why is he known as the "King of the North"?

I find them...: JD Vance clears air on whether Iran snubbed him during talksJD Vance said Iran's brief walkout from the Switzerland talks was not a snub. He said negotiations continued for hours despite online speculation and visible tensions.

Save me: Students trapped in bathrooms made desperate calls as Lucknow blaze ragedStudents trapped in bathrooms, desperate phone calls for help, a mother pleading to reach her son and survivors escaping through burning wires – eyewitnesses reveal the horrifying moments inside Lucknow's deadly blaze in which 15 people were killed.

Rebel TMC MLAs replace Mamata Banerjee as chairperson, pick Arup RoyFor a party founded by Mamata Banerjee in 1998 and built around her political identity for nearly three decades, the latest developments represent one of the most significant internal challenges in its history.

Monsoon rain across west coast, Central India to swelter on TuesdayThe southwest monsoon began to advance further on Monday, and is expected to stay on track. Here's what different parts of India are forecast to face on Tuesday, June 23.

Has Trump succeeded in making Vance the face of America's humiliation by Iran?US President Donald Trump on multiple occasions, albeit in jest, suggested that if the Iran talks failed, he would blame JD Vance for it. Trump sent Vance for negotiations to Geneva, and the developments suggest that the Vice President might actually be emerging as the face of America's humiliation by Iran.

Bihar on the boil over encounter killing of man who made anti-government postsBihar government has ordered a judicial inquiry into the Bhojpur encounter in which Bharat Bhushan Tiwari was killed. The move follows protests, questions over the police action and concern within the BJP.

6 PMs in 10 years: A timeline of Britain's political churnKeir Starmer said he will step down as Prime Minister once the Labour Party chooses a successor. His exit extends a decade of rapid leadership turnover, during which 6 prime ministers have resigned, contributing to a period of instability in British politics.

Pak anti-terror court sentences Baloch lioness Mahrang to life in jailAn anti-terrorism court in Quetta sentenced activist Mahrang Baloch and Balochistan leader Sibghatullah Shah to life imprisonment in the Gwadar rally case. The ruling has intensified the dispute around the trial, with the BYC denouncing it and continuing its boycott.

Iran's Switzerland snub of JD Vance, and Washington's humiliation is completeIranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi briefly walked out of the opening session of US-Iran talks in Switzerland after speaking to Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif. The episode and later shifts in Washington's stance sharpened the view that Tehran was negotiating from a position of strength.

6 rebel Uddhav Sena MPs join Eknath Shinde's faction after days of speculationThe induction of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs into Eknath Shinde's camp is being viewed as a major political gain for the ruling faction and a setback for Uddhav Thackeray.

Mamata's TMC to Uddhav Sena: Are rebels sugarcoating party splits now?From Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress to Uddhav Thackeray's Shiv Sena (UBT), recent defections to the BJP-led NDA have unfolded with an unusual lack of public hostility. Both the rebels and the leaders refrained from direct attacks on one another. Is India witnessing a new, sugar-coated style of political defection?

Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston's rise, dies at 94kicker - Clive Davis, the music executive who helped shape generations of stars, died on Monday at the age of 94. His six-decade career left a lasting imprint on pop, rock, jazz and R&B.

US notifies $482 million support deal for India's Apache helicopters, M777 howitzersThe US has notified a proposed USD 482.2 million sustainment support sale for India's Apache helicopters and M777A2 howitzers. The package underscores deeper defence cooperation while helping India maintain critical combat platforms.

Why are homeowners turning to thermocol wall technology for cooling?As temperatures continue to rise, EPS wall technology is drawing interest as a cooling solution for homes. Made with thermocol-based panels, the construction method aims to reduce heat transfer, improve insulation and lower energy use.

England name squad for T20I series against India, uncapped all-rounder called up England have named a 17-player squad for the T20I series against India, with James Coles receiving his first call-up. The selection also brings back Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood while injuries have ruled out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton.

Marketa Vondrousova hit with 4-year ban over doping test fiasco Former Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years after an independent tribunal ruled against her in an anti-doping test refusal case. The ITIA charged her for allegedly denying a doping officer entry to her home in December 2025.

ARG vs AUT Highlights: Messi double puts Argentina into Round of 32 Argentina (ARG) vs Austria (AUT) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi breaks the World Cup record and bags a brace as Argentina move into the Round of 32 with their 2-0 win against Austria.

Suryakumar Yadav draws parallels with Men's T20 WC win to inspire India Women Women's T20 World Cup 2026, INDW vs SAW: Suryakumar Yadav inspired the India Women's team by recalling India Men's turnaround in the T20 World Cup after a loss to South Africa.

Germany dealt major setback as Schlotterbeck ruled out for rest of FIFA World Cup Germany's FIFA World Cup campaign has suffered a major blow with defender Nico Schlotterbeck ruled out for the rest of the tournament and several months after sustaining a left ankle ligament injury during the 2-1 win over Ivory Coast.

MS Dhoni is Roger Federer of cricket, Virat Kohli mirrors Carlos Alcaraz: Sanju Samson Sanju Samson compared MS Dhoni to Roger Federer for his calm, tactical brilliance and Virat Kohli to Carlos Alcaraz for his explosive, high-intensity style. Alcaraz, a two-time Wimbledon champion, is set to miss the 2026 edition due to fitness concerns.

Gurpreet Sandhu on India missing FIFA World Cup: Why didn't we reach Asian Cup? Gurpreet Singh Sandhu sparked debate by saying India's World Cup absence reflects a deeper issue, failure to consistently qualify for and progress in the AFC Asian Cup, the key stepping stone in Asian football.

Ronaldo hat-trick against Uzbekistan? Michael Owen slams critics of Portugal star FIFA World Cup 2026: Michael Owen defended Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's draw with DR Congo and backed him to answer critics against Uzbekistan. His remarks come amid scrutiny over Ronaldo's form, Portugal's balance and Roberto Martínez's authority.

I'm a failure as captain: Chamari Athapaththu after Sri Lanka face early exit threatChamari Athapaththu was left emotional as Sri Lanka suffered a five-wicket defeat to the West Indies, leaving their Women's T20 World Cup campaign on the brink of an early exit. Athapaththu said she felt like a 'failure as captain' for not guiding Sri Lanka to a World Cup semi.

Motherhood and cricket can coexist, says ICC in landmark moveThe ICC has launched its Return to Play Post-Pregnancy Guidelines to support women cricketers returning to elite sport after childbirth through a structured, science-backed framework.

Mo Salah joins wild street party in Vancouver after Egypt's historic victoryMohamed Salah joined Egypt fans on Vancouver's streets after the 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand. The celebrations marked Egypt's first FIFA World Cup finals victory and the release of a 92-year wait.

How Egypt coach's pep talk inspired historic FIFA World Cup win vs New ZealandFIFA World Cup: Egypt coach Hossam Hassan revealed the stirring speech he delivered that helped Egypt recover from a 0-1 deficit to beat New Zealand 3-1 in the Group G match.

Constant stoppages make World Cup matches tougher: Scaloni on hydration breaksArgentina manager Lionel Scaloni has questioned FIFA's newly introduced hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, saying the stoppages disrupt the natural flow of matches and can alter tactical dynamics during games.

Messi, Mbappe and Haaland return to action, chasing more historyFIFA World Cup 2026: As football's ultimate heavyweights return for their second group fixtures, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland look to consolidate opening-day masterclasses and steer their nations toward early knockout qualification in North America.

Are Harmanpreet Kaur's India in danger of getting knocked out early?Women's T20 World Cup 2026: India's defeat to South Africa has turned their Group A campaign into a qualification battle. The result leaves Harmanpreet Kaur's side needing wins over Bangladesh and Australia to stay in semi-final contention.

ICC punishes England with 12-point WTC penalty after loss to New ZealandEngland's World Test Championship (WTC) hopes suffered a major setback after the International Cricket Council (ICC) docked the team 12 points for a slow over-rate during their 253-run defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viralIndia A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

Iran leave handwritten note in Los Angeles locker room and it has a plea for peaceFIFA World Cup: Iran's players left an emotional handwritten message in their SoFi Stadium locker room on Sunday, thanking Los Angeles for its hospitality and supporters for standing by the team during a challenging World Cup campaign.

Iran played beautiful game under worst conditions: Coach Amir hails Belgium drawIran coach Amir Ghalenoei hailed his side's performance after a hard-fought 0-0 draw against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup, saying his players produced a "beautiful game" despite battling what he described as the "worst possible conditions" heading into the tournament.

Ignore the numbers? India back clutch Jemimah Rodrigues to defy the critics againWomen's T20 World Cup: Amol Muzumdar has given his unflinching backing to the out-of-form Jemimah Rodrigues, labelling her a clutch match-winner as India face a crucial World Cup tightrope following defeat to South Africa.

Egypt end 92-year wait for win at FIFA World Cup as Salah buries New ZealandMohamed Salah inspired Egypt to a historic 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand on Sunday as the Pharaohs ended a 92-year wait for their first FIFA World Cup victory. Goals from Mostafa Ziko, Salah and Mahmoud Trezeguet secured the landmark result and sent Egypt to the top of Group G.

Cape Verde eye World Cup knockout berth after battling Uruguay draw: Coach BubistaCape Verde coach Bubista believes his side have earned the right to target a place in the World Cup knockout rounds after battling to a 2-2 draw with Uruguay, a result that kept the debutants firmly in contention for qualification from Group H.

After watching 2022 World Cup from a classroom, Yamal revels in dream debut goalLamine Yamal marked his first World Cup start with a goal as Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0. The strike completed a leap from watching the 2022 tournament in a classroom to shaping Spain's campaign on the pitch.

Vozinha's mum watches on as Cape Verde continue captivating journeyCape Verde's remarkable World Cup story continued on Sunday as they held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in Miami, with goalkeeper Vozinha's mother, Evora, watching from the stands. After missing her son's historic performance against Spain due to visa issues, Evora was finally able to attend following a successful fundraising effort.

1st WC goal, another point: Cape Verde's fairytale rolls on with Uruguay drawCape Verde's fairytale got a new chapter as the African side scored their first World Cup goal and secured another point with a 2-2 draw against Uruguay.

Who is Alireza Beiranvand? Iran's wall with two Guinness records stuns BelgiumFIFA World Cup 2026: Alireza Beiranvand added his name to the growing list of goalkeeper heroes at FIFA World Cup 2026 with a seven-save masterclass against Belgium. The Iranian shot-stopper, who holds two Guinness World Records, frustrated the Red Devils and kept Iran's knockout hopes alive with a crucial clean sheet.

NZ 1-3 EGY HIGHLIGHTS: Salah inspires Egypt's historic winNew Zealand (NZ) vs Egypt (EGY) HIGHLIGHTS, FIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt's wait for a World Cup victory is finally over. Mohamed Salah was once again the man for the big occasion as the Pharaohs beat New Zealand in a gripping Group G contest, before producing a determined defensive display to see out the closing stages. It is a result that will be celebrated across Egypt, with the national team securing its first-ever win on football's biggest stage.

The Beiranvand wall helps Iran secure vital point against 10-man BelgiumIran held 10-man Belgium to a goalless draw in Los Angeles with Alireza Beiranvand making a series of crucial saves. The point strengthened Iran's knockout hopes and exposed Belgium's wastefulness despite their control of the ball.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
